name: Safavid Iran (1501–1736)

colors.symbolism: Islam and the Shiʿa creed promoted as the state religion
the embroidered Lion and Sun device

description: A green field bearing a gold Lion and Sun (Shir-o-Khorshid) device — a couchant lion before a rising sun — embroidered in the centre.
history: Banner of Safavid Iran (1501–1736). Early Safavid standards under Ismail I bore a gold device on green; from the later 16th century the Lion and Sun became the established emblem, remaining in use to the end of the dynasty in 1736.

features.role: rising sun of the Shir-o-Khorshid emblem
